table.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; font-size: 11px; font-family: verdana; vertical-align: top;

tr.style: border: 1px #F0F0F0 solid; font-size: 11px; font-family: verdana; vertical-align: top

th.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : left; background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

a.title: The logical name of the element

th.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : left; background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

a.title: Information about the use of the element

th.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : left; background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

a.title: Minimum and Maximum # of times the element can appear in the instance
Reference to the type of the element

th.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : left; background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

a.title: Additional information about the element
Legend for this format

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: white

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : left; background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; white-space: nowrap; background-image: url(tbl_bck1.png)

a.title: B.1 - EHDS refined base model for Patient Summary body data elements

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : left; background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : left; background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

vertical-align: top; text-align : left; background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

tr.td:
Patient Summary Body
Instances of this logical model are not marked to be the target of a Reference

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: #F7F7F7

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : left; background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; white-space: nowrap; background-image: url(tbl_bck11.png)

a.title: Alert section includes information about allergies, intolerances and other substantial alerts or warnings that health professionals should be aware of.

a.title: This section may provide both synthetic anamnesis (e.g. description of phases of the pathology as a chronological summary of clustered clinical information) and anecdotal evidence that clinicians can collect from the patient, and can read in a narrative form.See Section 2, Chapter III, Art 7, (c).

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : left; background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

a.title: Major procedures.

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : left; background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

a.title: Need for the patient to be continuously assessed by third parties; functional status may influence decisions about how to plan and administer treatments.

a.title: Medication summary section

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : left; background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : left; background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: #F7F7F7

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : left; background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; white-space: nowrap; background-image: url(tbl_bck000.png)

a.title: Relevant prescribed medicines whose period of time indicated for the treatment has not yet expired whether it has been dispensed or not, or medicines that influence current health status or are relevant to a clinical decision.

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : left; background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

tr.td: B.1.4.1 - Current and relevant past medicines

a.title: Legend for this format

description.value: B.1 - EHDS refined base model for Patient Summary body data elements

definition.value: B.1 - EHDS refined base model for Patient Summary body data elements
Alert section includes information about allergies, intolerances and other substantial alerts or warnings that health professionals should be aware of.
A record of allergies and intolerances.
Medical alert information (other alerts not included in allergies).
Medical history
Vaccination / prophylaxis information
Resolved, closed or inactive problems
Medical history section

This section may provide both synthetic anamnesis (e.g. description of phases of the pathology as a chronological summary of clustered clinical information) and anecdotal evidence that clinicians can collect from the patient, and can read in a narrative form.See Section 2, Chapter III, Art 7, (c).
Medical problems section
Health conditions affecting the health of the patient and are important to be known for a health professional during a health encounter.
Medical devices and implants section.
Major procedures.
Need for the patient to be continuously assessed by third parties; functional status may influence decisions about how to plan and administer treatments.
Medication summary section

short.value: B.1.4.1 - Current and relevant past medicines

definition.value: Relevant prescribed medicines whose period of time indicated for the treatment has not yet expired whether it has been dispensed or not, or medicines that influence current health status or are relevant to a clinical decision.
B.1 - EHDS refined base model for Patient Summary body data elements
Alert section includes information about allergies, intolerances and other substantial alerts or warnings that health professionals should be aware of.
A record of allergies and intolerances.
Medical alert information (other alerts not included in allergies).
Medical history
Vaccination / prophylaxis information
Resolved, closed or inactive problems
Medical history section

This section may provide both synthetic anamnesis (e.g. description of phases of the pathology as a chronological summary of clustered clinical information) and anecdotal evidence that clinicians can collect from the patient, and can read in a narrative form.See Section 2, Chapter III, Art 7, (c).
Medical problems section
Health conditions affecting the health of the patient and are important to be known for a health professional during a health encounter.
Medical devices and implants section.
Major procedures.
Need for the patient to be continuously assessed by third parties; functional status may influence decisions about how to plan and administer treatments.
Medication summary section

short.value: B.1.4.1 - Current and relevant past medicines

definition.value: Relevant prescribed medicines whose period of time indicated for the treatment has not yet expired whether it has been dispensed or not, or medicines that influence current health status or are relevant to a clinical decision.
